ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the weather station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the weather station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the weather station */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the weather station (in meters) */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the station was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance check */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status (e.g., active, inactive, under maintenance) */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Type of data source (e.g., surface, radar, satellite, balloon) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_conditions" (
  "cond_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather condition */,
  "cond_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather condition */,
  "cond_description" TEXT /* Description of the weather condition */,
  "cond_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the weather condition (e.g., mild, moderate, severe) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cond_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "precipitation_types" (
  "precip_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each precipitation type */,
  "precip_name" TEXT /* Name of the precipitation type */,
  "precip_description" TEXT /* Description of the precipitation type */,
  "precip_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the precipitation (e.g., light, moderate, heavy) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("precip_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "wind_directions" (
  "wind_dir_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each wind direction */,
  "wind_dir_name" TEXT /* Name of the wind direction */,
  "wind_dir_description" TEXT /* Description of the wind direction */,
  "wind_speed_range" TEXT /* Range of wind speeds typically observed for the direction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("wind_dir_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cloud_types" (
  "cloud_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cloud type */,
  "cloud_name" TEXT /* Name of the cloud type */,
  "cloud_description" TEXT /* Description of the cloud type */,
  "cloud_height" REAL /* Typical height of the cloud type (in meters) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cloud_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "observation_types" (
  "obs_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each observation type */,
  "obs_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the observation type */,
  "obs_type_description" TEXT /* Description of the observation type */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the observation type (e.g., degrees Celsius, millimeters) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("obs_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_station_instruments" (
  "inst_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instrument */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station using the instrument */,
  "inst_name" TEXT /* Name of the instrument */,
  "inst_description" TEXT /* Description of the instrument */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the instrument (in percentage) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inst_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_station_instruments_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_station_observations" (
  "ws_obs_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each observation */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station making the observation */,
  "obs_id" INTEGER /* ID of the observation */,
  "obs_time" INTEGER /* Time of the observation */,
  "obs_value" REAL /* Value of the observation */,
  "instrument_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instrument used for the observation */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the dataset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ws_obs_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_station_observations_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_station_observations_instrument_id FOREIGN KEY ("instrument_id") REFERENCES weather_station_instruments ("inst_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., meteorologist, researcher, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "obs_id" INTEGER /* ID of the observation being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the data was accessed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the data was accessed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station making the forecast */,
  "forecast_time" INTEGER /* Time of the forecast */,
  "forecast_value" REAL /* Value of the forecast */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forecast model used */,
  "forecast_type" TEXT /* Type of forecast (e.g., temperature, precipitation) */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the forecast (in percentage) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES weather_forecast_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_forecast_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the forecast model */,
  "model_description" TEXT /* Description of the forecast model */,
  "resolution" REAL /* Spatial resolution of the model (in kilometers) */,
  "temporal_resolution" REAL /* Temporal resolution of the model (in hours) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)
